notes: Converted from the old chapter extraction schema.
Hidden facts, motives, and faction pressures require living-world re-extraction.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed package contains mostly public battle events (Fang Yuan breaking the primordial qi wall, Bei Yuan immortals attacking, Qin Dingling and Long Gong fighting Fang Yuan). No private knowledge, secret plans, or future-known information is directly supported by the source pointers. The pointers describe visible combat actions and character identities. No hidden fact candidate can be extracted without speculating beyond what the source directly supports.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed package contains battle events and entity relations but lacks direct observation of character motives. Most actions (e.g., 秦鼎菱 attacking 方源, 冰塞川接应方源) show behavior but the underlying motives are ambiguous - they could be duty, strategy, emotion, etc. No seed fact directly supports a specific inferred motive. Per rules, when only behavior is visible and motive is ambiguous, return empty characterMotives.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed contains multiple individuals (方源, 龙公, 秦鼎菱, 冰塞川, etc.) but no named collective faction/group with a concrete stake in the seed facts. '北原诸仙' (Northern Plains Immortals) is mentioned as a loose grouping but is not a named faction with organizational structure in this seed. '天庭' (Heavenly Court) is mentioned but only through individual members (龙公, 紫薇仙子, 秦鼎菱) acting individually, not as a collective faction pressure. '长生天' is referenced in a relation but has no direct stake in the events of this chapter. No named family, clan, shop, hall, or organization appears with a clear collective stake that would justify a faction pressure candidate.
IF export filter quarantined 6 legacy old-schema branches.
